prompt: Explain photosynthesis to a curious adult non-scientist in around 150 words. Avoid a bulleted list.
response: Photosynthesis is a process that occurs within plants and some microorganisms to convert light energy into chemical energy by generating glucose from carbon dioxide and water. In essence, plants absorb or “capture” sunlight in summer and spring to create their food in summer but also in winter, unlike what some people mistakenly believe. Photosynthesis occurs predominantly in the foliage of plants or the microscopic objects called cyanobacteria for very simple living beings. When the sunlight shines onto a leaf’s surface in summer and spring it contains within it many tiny pores or sockets named stomata or the equivalent in cyanobacteria and those are basically the location or the site where photosynthesis happens. Through the action of a molecule called chlorophyll, the light energy is converted into chemical energy in the form of glucose sugar, which the plant can then use for its living purposes, but also which can then be transported to other parts of the plant and stored to create new cells for will provide special challenge or will become part of the tree's trunk, for its branches, and for its leaves. It’s the most important biological process for all life on earth, for without photosynthesis there would simply be no life on earth’s surface. And that’s why photosynthesis is fundamentally critical for every single body that exists on planet earth, because it is through this incredible process that the energy of sunlight is converted into chemical energy that then becomes the fuel that is required for life for every single living creature that exists on planet earth. Without access to and utilization of that energy provided by photosynthesis, there simply would be no life on planet earth. It is one of the most important concepts every single adult needs to understand, because without it, there simply would be no life on planet earth. And that’s why photosynthesis is fundamentally critical for every single body that exists on planet earth, because it is through this incredible process that the energy of sunlight is converted into chemical energy that then becomes the fuel that is required for life for every single living creature that exists on planet earth. Without access to and utilization of that energy provided by photosynthesis, there simply would be no life on planet earth. It is one of the most important concepts every single adult needs to understand, because without it, there simply would be no life on planet earth. And that’s why every single adult needs to understand photosynthesis. So in summary, do you want to know what photosynthesis is? This mysterious term refers to the manner by which plants take in sunlight in summer and spring and then convert that sunlight into glucose, which it uses to create the various food that nourishes the plant. Now, you may be asking yourself, "Well, how does the plant take [this action of] photosynthesis? What precisely is the process for how photosynthesis works to achieve that action"?
